paths:
{{#each security_paths}}
  - "{{this}}"
{{/each}}
default_severity: error
---

# Security Rules
<!-- Generated by Clauding Thought /init -- do not edit the structure, but you may add rules -->
<!-- Severity: error = active vulnerability (BLOCK), warning = potential risk (WARN), info = informational -->

## Posture: {{security_posture}}

<!-- 'strict' = all violations BLOCK. 'advisory' = new code BLOCKs, known gaps WARN. -->
<!-- Security is non-negotiable regardless of posture. Even non-public apps need strict  -->
<!-- practices: exposure status changes, internal threats exist, compliance audits happen. -->
<!-- Advisory is the on-ramp toward strict -- not a permanent exemption. -->

{{#if baseline_gaps}}
## Baseline Gaps (tracked debt)

These are known security gaps inherited from the existing codebase. New code in these
areas MUST follow strict rules. Existing code is flagged as WARN with a migration path.

| ID | Category | Severity | Scope | Status |
|----|----------|----------|-------|--------|
{{#each baseline_gaps}}
| {{id}} | {{category}} | {{severity}} | {{scope}} | {{status}} |
{{/each}}

See `manifest.security.baseline_gaps` for mitigation details.
{{/if}}

## Authentication
<!-- severity: error -->
<!-- scope: {{controller_paths}} -->

<!-- Derived from manifest.architecture.auth -->

- Every public controller method MUST have authorization
- Pattern: `{{auth_pattern}}`
- Example: `{{auth_example}}`

## Tenant Isolation
<!-- severity: error -->
<!-- scope: {{model_paths}}, {{validation_paths}} -->

<!-- Derived from manifest.architecture.tenancy -- omit this section if tenancy.strategy is "none" -->

- Every model with `{{scope_column}}` MUST use `{{trait_path}}`
- `Rule::exists()` and `Rule::unique()` MUST include `->where('{{scope_column}}', ...)`
- NEVER use `withoutGlobalScopes()` unless explicitly required and documented
- NEVER use `Model::all()` -- always use `Model::query()->...`

## Data Exposure
<!-- severity: error -->
<!-- scope: {{controller_paths}}, {{resource_paths}} -->

<!-- Derived from manifest.security.sensitive_fields -->

- Never expose these fields to the frontend: `{{sensitive_fields}}`
- Serialization approach: `{{api_style}}`
- Raw models must never be passed to view/response layer

## Injection Prevention
<!-- severity: error -->

- No raw SQL without bound parameters (`DB::statement`, `whereRaw` -> must use `?` bindings)
- No unsanitized user input in: file paths, shell commands, external API calls
- No string interpolation in query builders

## Secrets
<!-- severity: error -->

- No hardcoded credentials, API keys, or tokens in source files
- Environment values only accessed through config files, never direct `env()` in application code

## File Access
<!-- severity: warning -->
<!-- scope: {{storage_paths}} -->

<!-- Derived from manifest.architecture.file_storage -->

- Storage disk: `{{file_disk}}`
- Authorization MUST be verified before streaming file downloads
- Log downloads when audit trail is required

## Custom Checks

<!-- Add project-specific security checks below. Format: -->
<!-- severity: error|warning|info -->
<!-- detect: description of what to look for -->
